section_title: Canadian P&C Banking: Continued volume growth in loans and deposits
section_statement: Canadian P&C Banking delivered resilient performance with record auto finance originations and double-digit growth in proprietary mortgage channels despite tariff uncertainty weighing on housing markets. The bank continues to focus on deepening customer relationships across its 15 million Canadian customers while maintaining profitability discipline.

quotes.quote_text: We look forward to Q3, we again expect NIM to be relatively stable.
2.82%, up 1 bp QoQ, primarily driven by higher loan margins. Guidance for Q3 is for NIM to remain relatively stable.

We would have seen moderate foreign currency spend, both on the debit and credit side, as consumers had a little more caution, especially with cross border purchases. We saw some lower merchant acquiring revenue in the business bank, and some small timing items.
This quarter, tariff uncertainty weighed on the Canadian housing market and we saw slower purchase activity.

content.subsection: Product Performance Highlights - TD Auto Finance

quotes.quote_text: In TD Auto Finance, we had record Q2 originations, which may reflect customers pulling forward auto purchases in anticipation of tariffs.

In Real Estate Secured Lending, we continue to enhance speed to decision and to provide tailored customer advice by referring more complex deals to our mobile mortgage specialist. As you know, our single greatest opportunity is to deepen relationships with our more than 15 million customers in Canada. We are executing against that, with continued strong referrals to the Business Bank and wealth.

As for the drivers of the QoQ decline in the mortgage business: if I look at our specific competitive position, we're a multi-channel through the cycle lender. I would say in this market, our broker originations have moderated a little bit. We have really strong relationships and we continue to compete for profitable business.
quotes.sentiment_rationale: Balanced competitive positioning with profitability focus

quotes.quote_text: I'm really pleased with the strength that we've seen in our proprietary channels. So if I look at our branch and MMS, proprietary ecosystem, originations, and again, this despite the macro environment, originations in those two channels are actually up double digits year over year. And so while you see a flat average sequential growth on a spot basis, we're actually up $1.5BN quarter over quarter. So the growth is there. The profile was a little different this quarter where we had higher paydowns in January and February, but then we exited the quarter with momentum. And overall, I think the fundamentals are very good. We are highly focused on building speed in our ecosystem, ensuring we have specialization. Our branch mortgage referral ecosystem, I had shared on the Q1 call, we had tripled referrals in that ecosystem in Q1, and that has continued in Q2.
quotes.sentiment_rationale: Strong proprietary channel performance despite macro headwinds

quotes.quote_text: When asked to elaborate on the higher paydowns: our book skews slightly more premium, and so as you have customers that have more disposable income by way of year-end bonuses, for example, and more liquidity in the market, we actually saw January and February prepayments were up double digits on a year-over-year basis. So that was some of the dampness in late in Q1, and then also the jump-off point as well as early in Q2 in February. So those are perhaps a little bit more symptomatic of our premium book.
quotes.sentiment_rationale: Premium customer behavior creating timing effects

content.subsection: Loan Growth Strategy and Profitability Focus

quotes.quote_text: Thoughts on approaching loan growth while protecting margins and profitability: profitability should always be a factor, and so we will compete to win profitable business and then leverage our strength in channels where we can differentiate on speed and customer experience, but profitability is important.
